KPI160
==================================================

.. math::

   \text{}

.. raw:: html

   <style>
      .kpi-table {
         width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin-bottom: 1.5em;
         border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; table-layout: fixed;
      }
      .kpi-table td {
         padding: 12px; vertical-align: top; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0;
         word-wrap: break-word;
      }
      .kpi-table td:first-child {
         width: 25%; font-weight: bold; background-color: #f7f7f7;
      }
      .kpi-table ul { list-style-type: disc; padding-left: 20px; margin: 0; }

         text-align: center; margin: 1em 0; overflow-x: auto; padding: 5px;
      }
      .kpi-table .formula-notes li { margin-bottom: 0.5em; }

   <div style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap;">
      <span style="font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; color: #4a5568;">Open in:</span>
      <a href="vscode://file/:0" target="_blank" class="btn btn-vscode">VS Code</a>
      <a href="pycharm://open?file=&line=0" target="_blank" class="btn btn-pycharm">PyCharm</a>
      <a href="/edit?kpi=KPI160" target="_blank" class="btn btn-edit">Edit</a>
   </div>

   <table class="kpi-table">
      <tbody>
         <tr>
            <td>Description</td>
            <td><ul><li>KPI160 measures the percentage of working capital relative to sales revenue</li><li>providing insight into the liquidity and operational efficiency of a business</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Objective</td>
            <td><ul><li>The objective of KPI160 is to assess how effectively a company is utilizing its working capital to generate sales</li><li>indicating financial health and operational performance</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Formula</td>
            <td>
               <p>The formula for KPI160 calculates the ratio of working capital to sales revenue, expressed as a percentage, to evaluate the relationship between a company&#x27;s short-term assets and its revenue generation.</p>
               <div class="math-equation">$$ \mathit{Wcto\,Sales\,Ratio} = \frac{\mathit{Working\,Capital}}{\mathit{Sales\,Revenue}} \times 100 $$</div><ul><li><i>Working Capital:</i> The numerator of the fraction.</li><li><i>Sales Revenue:</i> The denominator of the fraction.</li></ul>
            </td>
         </tr>

         <tr>
            <td>Generation time</td>
            <td>
               9.0s (AI 9.0s + Render 0.0s)
               <div style="font-size: 12px; color: #6b7280; margin-top: 4px;">
                  Total: 9.028s
                  • AI: 9.025s
                  • Render: 0.002s
               </div>
            </td>
         </tr>

         <tr>
            <td>Extraction rate %</td>
            <td>100.0%</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Error rate %</td>
            <td>0.0%</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Used in KPI(s)</td>
            <td><ul><li>KPI160 is utilized in financial analysis and performance assessment to monitor liquidity and operational efficiency</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Input Measure</td>
            <td><ul><li>The input measures for this KPI include working capital and sales revenue</li><li>both of which are critical for understanding a company&#x27</li><li>s financial position</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Unit of Measure</td>
            <td>The unit of measure for KPI160 is a percentage, reflecting the proportion of working capital to sales revenue.</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Reporting Source</td>
            <td>The reporting source for KPI160 is the finance department, which provides the necessary financial data for calculation.</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Comments</td>
            <td>A higher percentage indicates better liquidity and efficiency in using working capital to support sales, while a lower percentage may suggest potential liquidity issues or inefficiencies.</td>
         </tr>
      </tbody>
   </table>

.. admonition:: Code Context
   :class: dropdown

   
   
   

   .. code-block:: sql
      :linenos:
   

      -- KPI160: % Working Capital / Sales Revenue
   SELECT
       (WorkingCapital / SalesRevenue) * 100 AS WCtoSalesRatio
   FROM finance;
